description_long: This is a wooden staircase-style ladder with five wide, flat steps and solid side panels, constructed from light-colored, knotty pine wood. The steps are evenly spaced and supported by thick, rectangular side rails that extend above the top step, forming short posts. The ladder's design is robust, with each step securely fitted into the side panels, and the wood grain and knots are clearly visible throughout. The side panels are solid, providing additional structural support and a sense of enclosure along the sides. There are no visible safety features such as anti-slip treads or handrails beyond the side panels themselves. The overall appearance is utilitarian and sturdy, suitable for use in garages, workshops, or as a fixed access stair in an urban environment.
description: A sturdy, light-colored wooden staircase-style ladder with five wide steps and solid side panels, made from knotty pine. The design is robust and utilitarian, with visible wood grain and no additional safety features.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five wide steps and the solid side panels extending above the top step.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thickness of the side panels and the depth of the steps.
description_view_2: Top-down view showing the flat upper surface and the rectangular posts at the ends of the side panels.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the solid construction and step arrangement.
